135	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — algebraic generic freeness (finite case)
136	
137	This section builds the **module-theoretic** generic-freeness statements that
138	underlie the geometric `genericFlatness` (blueprint
139	`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure~\S4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness").
140	
141	The full algebraic statement — `A` a noetherian domain, `B` a *finite-type*
142	`A`-algebra, `M` a finite `B`-module ⟹ `∃ f ≠ 0` with `M_f` free over `A_f` —
143	is a deep theorem (prime-filtration dévissage + Noether normalisation +
144	clearing denominators). Mathlib already supplies most of the dévissage
145	machinery (`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`,
146	`ModuleCat.free_shortExact`, `exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg`), but it does
147	**not** yet contain the polynomial-ring core (generic freeness for a finite
148	module over `A[X₁,…,X_d]`). See the file `task_results` handoff for the
149	precise decomposition of the remaining gap.
150	
151	What we *can* land axiom-clean here is the **finite-module / finite-morphism
152	case**: when `M` is finite as an `A`-module (in particular when `B` is
153	module-finite over `A`), generic freeness follows directly from
154	`Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers` applied at the
155	generic point `Frac A`, where `M ⊗_A Frac A` is a finite vector space hence
156	free. This is a genuine special case of the algebraic generic-freeness
157	theorem (the case of a *finite* morphism `X → S`), and a reusable building
158	block for the dévissage's leaves. -/
159	
160	namespace GenericFreeness
161	
162	/-- **Generic freeness, finite-module case.** For a noetherian integral domain
163	`A` and a finite `A`-module `M`, there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that the
164	localisation `M_f` is free over `A_f = Localization.Away f`.
165	
166	This is the `d = 0` (finite-morphism) special case of the algebraic
167	generic-flatness theorem (`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure~\S4):
168	inverting the generic point `Frac A`, the localised module is a finite vector
169	space hence free, and `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers`
170	descends that freeness to a single basic open `D(f) ⊆ Spec A`. -/
171	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite
172	    (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
173	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M] :
174	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
175	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
176	  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation A M := Module.finitePresentation_of_finite A M
177	  obtain ⟨r, hr, hfree, _⟩ :=
178	    Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers (nonZeroDivisors A)
179	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (nonZeroDivisors A) M) (FractionRing A)
180	  exact ⟨r, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hr, hfree⟩
181	
182	/-- **Generic flatness, finite-module case.** The flatness form of
183	`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`: for a noetherian domain `A` and a
184	finite `A`-module `M`, there is a non-zero `f` with `M_f` flat over `A_f`.
185	This is the affine-local content of `genericFlatness` for a finite morphism. -/
186	theorem exists_flat_localizationAway_of_finite
187	    (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
188	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M] :
189	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
190	      Module.Flat (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
191	  obtain ⟨f, hf, hfree⟩ := exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
192	  haveI := hfree
193	  exact ⟨f, hf, Module.Flat.of_free⟩
194	
195	/-- **Generic freeness, finite-morphism case.** If `A` is a noetherian domain,
196	`B` a *module-finite* `A`-algebra, and `M` a finite `B`-module (with the
197	compatible `A`-module structure), then there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` with `M_f`
198	free over `A_f`. Reduces to `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` via
199	`Module.Finite.trans` (a finite module over a module-finite algebra is finite
200	over the base). This is generic flatness for a *finite* morphism `X → S`. -/
201	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_moduleFinite
202	    (A : Type*) (B : Type*) (M : Type*)
203	    [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
204	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Module.Finite A B]
205	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
206	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
207	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
208	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
209	  haveI : Module.Finite A M := Module.Finite.trans B M
210	  exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
211	
212	end GenericFreeness
213	
214	namespace Scheme
215	
216	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
217	
218	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
219	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
220	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
221	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
222	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
223	
224	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
225	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
226	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
227	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
228	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
229	
230	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
231	
232	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
233	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
234	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
235	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
236	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
237	morphism of `f`).
238	
239	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
240	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
241	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
242	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
243	the flattening-stratification theorem.
244	
245	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
246	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
247	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
248	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
249	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
250	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
251	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
252	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
253	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
254	
255	end Scheme
256	
257	/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
258	
259	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
260	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive
261	engine of the flattening-stratification theorem: combined with
262	Noetherian induction on the closed complement `S ∖ V`, it produces the
263	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.
264	
265	Algebraically (theorem `generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin): for a
266	noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
267	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
268	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
269	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on
270	each finite-type-algebra patch of `X` above `Spec A`.
271	
272	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/
273	
274	/-- **Generic flatness theorem** (Nitsure §4 / Stacks ?).
275	
276	For a noetherian integral scheme `S`, a finite-type morphism `p : X ⟶ S`,
277	and a coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a non-empty open subscheme
278	`V ⊆ S` such that `𝓕|_{X_V} = 𝓕|_{p⁻¹V}` is flat over `𝓞_V`.
279	
280	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §4: pass to a non-empty affine open
281	`Spec A ⊆ S` where `A` is a noetherian domain, then apply the algebraic
282	form (Noether normalisation + Auslander–Buchsbaum-style filtration
283	argument) to each finite-type-`A`-algebra `B` arising from an affine
284	cover of `p⁻¹(Spec A)`. The witness `V` is the common basic open
285	`D(f_1 f_2 ⋯ f_r) ⊆ Spec A` clearing the finitely many
286	generic-flatness elements `f_i ∈ A` produced on each patch. -/
287	theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
288	    (p : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules) :
289	    ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧
290	      ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (_ : U ≤ V) {W : X.Opens}
291	        (_ : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U),
292	        letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
293	        Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := by
294	  sorry
295	
296	/-! ## §3. Sub-lemmas of the existence proof
297	
298	The Nitsure §4 proof of the existence theorem decomposes into three
299	sub-lemmas wired together by Noetherian induction. The first is the
300	special case `n = 0` (when `ℙⁿ_S = S`); the second is the
301	Noetherian-induction reduction to a finite stratification where `𝓕`
302	*already* becomes flat (but without polynomial indexing); the third is
303	the assembly step that re-introduces the Hilbert-polynomial indexing
304	via the direct images `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`.
305	
306	Blueprint references: `lem:flat_locus_open`, `lem:nonflat_locus_proper`,
307	`lem:noetherian_induction_strata`. -/
308	
309	/-- **Lemma 5 (special case `n = 0`).** [Nitsure §4 special case]
310	
311	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` a coherent `𝓞_S`-module, there exists a
312	countable family `S_e ⊆ S` (indexed by `e ∈ ℕ`) of locally-closed
313	subschemes such that
314	- each `S_e` is a (locally-closed) immersion `S_e ⟶ S`;
315	- the underlying sets `|S_e|` partition `|S|`;
316	- the pullback `𝓕|_{S_e}` is flat over `𝓞_{S_e}` (an `𝓞_{S_e}`-module
317	  pulled back is automatically `𝓞_{S_e}`-flat over itself, which is the
318	  substantive content for this lemma; the *rank*-`e` refinement is
319	  iter-177+ work once the locally-free-of-rank-`e` predicate is in
320	  scope).
321	
322	The function `s ↦ dim_{κ(s)} 𝓕|_s` is upper-semicontinuous (this is
323	content (iii) of Nitsure's special case), encoded indirectly by the
324	fact that the strata are locally-closed and disjoint.
325	
326	Nitsure's proof: Nakayama produces a local presentation
327	`𝓞_V^{⊕m} ⟶ 𝓞_V^{⊕e} ⟶ 𝓕|_V ⟶ 0`; the closed subscheme `V_e ⊆ V`
328	defined by the matrix-entry ideal of the first map represents the
329	locus of locally-free-of-rank-`e`; gluing over a cover and intersecting
330	with `V ∖ ⋃_{e' > e} V_{e'}` produces the locally-closed `S_e`. -/
331	lemma flatLocusStratification {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
332	    (F : S.Modules) :
333	    ∃ (S_ : ℕ → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ e, S_ e ⟶ S),
334	      (∀ e, IsImmersion (ι e)) ∧
335	      (∀ e e', e ≠ e' → Disjoint (Set.range (ι e).base) (Set.range (ι e').base)) ∧
336	      (∀ s : S, ∃ e, s ∈ Set.range (ι e).base) ∧
337	      (∀ e, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (ι e) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (ι e)).obj F)) := by
338	  sorry
339	
340	/-- **Lemma 6 (Noetherian-induction reduction).** [Nitsure §4 general
341	case opening]
342	
343	For `S` noetherian, `π : X ⟶ S` proper, and `𝓕` coherent on `X`, there
344	exist finitely many reduced locally-closed immersions `V_i ⟶ S` with
345	pairwise disjoint set-theoretic images covering `|S|`, such that the
346	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S V_i}` is flat over `V_i`.
347	
348	Nitsure's proof: peel off non-empty open flat patches by
349	`genericFlatness` applied to each irreducible component (with its
350	reduced subscheme structure), induct on the closed complement
351	`S ∖ V` (which is again noetherian, hence the induction terminates by
352	Noetherianity).
353	
354	The substantive content over the special case (Lemma 5) is that the
355	strata `V_i` are *reduced* and that `𝓕` becomes flat above them (not
356	merely that strata exist); the polynomial-indexed refinement of the
357	main theorem (`flatteningStratification`) requires further assembly
358	(`flatLocusAssembly`). -/
359	lemma flatLocusReduction {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
360	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
361	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (V_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ i, V_ i ⟶ S),
362	      (∀ i, IsImmersion (ι i)) ∧
363	      (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (Set.range (ι i).base) (Set.range (ι j).base)) ∧
364	      (∀ s : S, ∃ i, s ∈ Set.range (ι i).base) ∧

366	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι i))).obj F)) := by

370	
371	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there
372	exists an integer `N` such that iterating the `n=0` stratification
373	(Lemma 5) on the direct images `E_i := π_*𝓕(N+i)` on `S`
374	(`i = 0, 1, …`) produces, after finitely many refinements, the
375	Hilbert-polynomial-indexed stratification of the main theorem.

377	The substantive content is the existence of the uniform vanishing

379	locally-closed strata `S_f` from the rank-strata `W_{e_0, …, e_n}`.

389	lemma flatLocusAssembly {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
390	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :

397	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) ∧

401	/-! ## §4. Existence of the flattening stratification (Nitsure §4 main)
402	
403	The main theorem combines the three sub-lemmas above: starting from a
404	proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S` and a coherent sheaf `𝓕`, one obtains a
405	finite locally-closed stratification of `S` indexed by Hilbert
406	polynomials, on each piece of which `𝓕` becomes flat, with all four
407	properties of Nitsure's Theorem packaged in a single conjunction.
408	
409	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_exists`
410	(Nitsure §4; Stacks 052H). -/
411	
412	/-- **Flattening stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4 main /
413	Stacks 052H].
414	
415	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S`, and a
416	coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a finite locally-closed
417	stratification `{S_f}` of `S` indexed by a finite set `I` such that
418	- each `ι : S_f ⟶ S` is a (locally-closed) immersion;
419	- the underlying sets `|S_f|` partition `|S|` (disjoint and covering);
420	- the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` is flat over `S_f` for each `f`.
421	
422	The (intended) substantive refinement is that the index set `I` is in
423	bijection with the set of Hilbert polynomials arising on fibres, and
424	that each `S_f` is uniquely determined by its Hilbert polynomial. For
425	the iter-176 file-skeleton the substantive type captures the
426	stratification + flatness; the Hilbert-polynomial labeling is encoded
427	in `flatLocusAssembly`'s `P : I → ℕ → ℤ` injection but elided here for
428	type-clarity.
429	
430	iter-177+: the body assembles `flatLocusReduction` (Lemma 6) ⟶
431	`flatLocusAssembly` (Lemma 7). The structural skeleton is encoded
432	directly here; the Mathlib-side ingredients required (relative
433	projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity, direct image
434	base-change `R^r π_* 𝓕(m) = 0` for `m ≫ 0`, cohomology-and-base-change
435	Stacks tag 02KH for the `H^0` form) are itemised in the blueprint
436	chapter §`Mathlib status`. -/
437	theorem flatteningStratification {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
438	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
439	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S),
440	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
441	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
442	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
443	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
444	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) := by
445	  sorry
446	
447	/-! ## §5. Universal property of the stratification
448	
449	Nitsure's part (ii): the morphism `i : ⨿_f S_f → S` assembled from the
450	stratum inclusions is universal for "`𝓕` becomes `T`-flat after pullback
451	along `T → S`". Equivalently, for any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that
452	`𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat, `φ` factors uniquely through some `S_f`.
453	
454	For the iter-176 file-skeleton we encode the universal property in the
455	factor-through form (∃! factorisation through one of the strata
456	`ι f : S_f ⟶ S`), which is the substantive Yoneda-bijection content of
457	Nitsure's part (ii).
458	
459	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_universal`
460	(Nitsure §4 (ii); Stacks 052H). -/
461	
462	/-- **Universal property of the flattening stratification**
463	[Nitsure §4 (ii) / Stacks 052H].
464	
465	Given the flattening stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (existence supplied
466	by `flatteningStratification`), a morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that the
467	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat factors uniquely through one of the
468	locally-closed inclusions `ι : S_f ⟶ S`.
469	
470	The hypothesis takes the stratification data `(I, S_, ι)` as an
471	explicit parameter (rather than re-extracting it from
472	`flatteningStratification`'s existential output) so the universal
473	property is decoupled from the choice of stratification — different
474	witnesses agree on the universal-property content. iter-177+: refine
475	to a `Functor.RepresentableBy`-style universal arrow once the
476	contravariant functor `T ↦ {φ : T ⟶ S | 𝓕|_{X_T} is T-flat}` is
477	available. -/
478	theorem flatteningStratification_universal {S X : Scheme.{u}}
479	    [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules)
480	    (I : Type u) [Finite I] (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
481	    [∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)] :
482	    ∀ {T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T ⟶ S),
483	      Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π φ)
484	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π φ)).obj F) →
485	      ∃! (data : (f : I) × (T ⟶ S_ f)),
486	        data.2 ≫ ι data.1 = φ := by
487	  sorry
488	
489	/-! ## §6. Curve specialisation (Route A consumer)
490	
491	In the Route A consumer setting, the input morphism is the second
492	projection `pr_T : C ×_k T ⟶ T` of the pullback of a smooth proper
493	curve `C/k` against a noetherian `k`-scheme `T`. A smooth proper curve
494	over a field is automatically projective (Hartshorne II.6.7 / II.7.6;
495	folklore), hence in particular proper; the relative-curve `C ×_k T → T`
496	inherits properness by base change, hence the hypotheses of the main
497	theorem are met. This corollary records the specialisation
498	explicitly. -/
499	
500	/-- **Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative
501	curve** [Nitsure §4 corollary; Route~A consumer A.2.a entry-point].
502	
503	Let `k` be a field, `C` a smooth proper curve over `k` (encoded as
504	`C : Over (Spec k)` with `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]` and
505	`[IsProper C.hom]`), and `T` a noetherian `k`-scheme. For any coherent
506	sheaf `𝓕` on the relative curve `C ×_k T`, the flattening
507	stratification conclusion of `flatteningStratification` applies to
508	`π = pr_T : C ×_k T → T` and `𝓕`: there is a finite locally-closed
509	stratification `{T_f ⊆ T}` set-theoretically covering `T` disjointly,
510	such that `𝓕|_{C ×_k T_f}` is flat over `T_f` for each `f`.
511	
512	iter-177+: the body invokes `flatteningStratification` on the
513	base-changed morphism `pullback.snd C.hom T.hom : (C ×_k T) ⟶ T`,
514	using that `IsProper (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom)` holds by base change
515	of `IsProper C.hom`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a
516	typed `sorry`. -/
517	theorem flatteningStratification.ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k]
518	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
519	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
520	    (T : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsLocallyNoetherian T.left]
521	    (F : (Limits.pullback C.hom T.hom).Modules) :
522	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (T_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, T_ f ⟶ T.left),
523	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
524	      (∀ t : T.left, ∃ f, t ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
525	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
526	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat
527	        (pullback.snd (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))
528	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
529	          (pullback.fst (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))).obj F)) := by
530	  sorry
531	
532	end AlgebraicGeometry

67	/-! ## §0. Presheaf-level: the dual of the monoidal unit is the unit
68	
69	Project-local supplement to `PresheafInternalHom.lean`: `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`
70	(the evaluation-at-`1` isomorphism `ℋom(𝟙_, 𝟙_) ≅ 𝟙_`), built over a general single-universe
71	base category.  It feeds `Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso` (below) at `R₀ := Y.presheaf`. -/
72	
73	namespace PresheafOfModules
74	
75	open InternalHom Opposite
76	
77	variable {D : Type u} [Category.{u, u} D] {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
78	
79	/-- **Section equivalence for the dual of the unit.** At an object `X`, endomorphisms of the
80	(restricted) unit `restr X 𝟙_ ⟶ restr X 𝟙_` are identified `R₀(X)`-linearly with `R₀(X)` itself,
81	via evaluation at `1`; the inverse is multiplication by a global scalar (`globalSMul`). The
82	substantive content is `left_inv`: every endomorphism of the unit is multiplication by its value
83	at `1` (proved from `φ`-naturality toward the terminal object of the slice). -/
84	noncomputable def unitDualSectionEquiv (X : Dᵒᵖ) :
85	    letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
86	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
87	      (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
88	    (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
89	        restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
90	      ≃ₗ[(R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u)] (R₀.obj (op X.unop) : Type u) := by
91	  letI := internalHomObjModule X.unop
92	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
93	    (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
94	  exact
95	    { toFun := fun φ =>
96	        evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
97	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
98	      map_add' := fun φ φ' => rfl
99	      map_smul' := fun c φ => by
100	        exact DFunLike.congr_fun (evalLin_smul _ X c φ)
101	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
102	      invFun := fun r =>
103	        globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
104	          (restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r
105	      left_inv := fun φ => by
106	        ext Y
107	        dsimp only
108	        erw [globalSMul_hom_apply]
109	        have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply φ (Over.mkIdTerminal.from Y.unop).op
110	          (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
111	        erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at hnat
112	        erw [hnat, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
113	        rfl
114	      right_inv := fun r => by
115	        change ((globalSMul Over.mkIdTerminal
116	            (restr X.unop
117	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) r).app
118	            (op (Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)))).hom
119	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)) = r
120	        rw [globalSMul_hom_apply, termRingMap_terminal]
121	        exact mul_one r }
122	
123	/-- **The presheaf dual of the monoidal unit is the unit**, `PresheafOfModules.dual 𝟙_ ≅ 𝟙_`,
124	assembled sectionwise from `unitDualSectionEquiv` with the evaluation-at-`1` naturality (mirroring
125	`InternalHom.internalHomEval`'s naturality at `M = 𝟙_`). -/
126	noncomputable def dualUnitIsoGen :
127	    PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := R₀)
128	        (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
129	      ≅ 𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :=
130	  PresheafOfModules.isoMk (fun X => (unitDualSectionEquiv X).toModuleIso)
131	    (fun {X Y} f => by
132	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext (LinearMap.ext fun φ => ?_)
133	      change evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
134	            ((PresheafOfModules.dual
135	              (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
136	            (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj Y : Type u))
137	          = ((𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f).hom
138	              (evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) X φ
139	                (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u)))
140	      have key := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply
141	        (φ : restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) ⟶
142	          restr X.unop (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))))
143	        (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
144	        (1 : ((R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).obj X : Type u))
145	      have hrm : (restr X.unop
146	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map
147	          (Over.homMk f.unop : Over.mk f.unop ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X.unop)).op
148	          = (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).map f := rfl
149	      rw [hrm] at key
150	      erw [PresheafOfModules.unit_map_one] at key
151	      have hAB : (op (Over.mk (𝟙 Y.unop ≫ f.unop)) : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ) = op (Over.mk f.unop) :=
152	        congrArg op (congrArg Over.mk (Category.id_comp f.unop))
153	      have homAppHEq : ∀ {A B : (Over X.unop)ᵒᵖ} (_ : A = B), HEq (φ.app A) (φ.app B) := by
154	        intro A B h; subst h; rfl
155	      have hdt : evalLin (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))) Y
156	          ((PresheafOfModules.dual
157	            (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))).map f φ)
158	          = (φ.app (op (Over.mk f.unop))).hom :=
159	        congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (eq_of_heq (homAppHEq hAB))
160	      exact (DFunLike.congr_fun hdt _).trans key)
161	
162	end PresheafOfModules

170	/-! ## §A. The C-bridge: restriction commutes with the sheaf-level dual -/
171	
172	open Opposite in
173	/-- **Leg-B atomic claim: the lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars` along the open-immersion

302	    -- so post-composing it with `(f.appIso P).hom` is the identity on `𝒪_X(f''ᵁP)`.  This is the
303	    -- load-bearing ring identity that collapses the double `restrictScalars` in the reverse
304	    -- component (`?collapse`); it is FALSE for an arbitrary `β`, hence supplied as a hypothesis and
305	    -- discharged at the unique caller (`sliceDualTransport.invFun`) via `Iso.hom_inv_id`.
306	    (hβ : ∀ (P : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y),
307	        ((β.app (op P)).hom).comp ((Scheme.Hom.appIso f P).hom.hom) = RingHom.id _)
308	    (ψ : (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj M.val).dual.obj V : Type u)) :
309	    (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj M.val.dual).obj V : Type u) := by
310	  refine { app := fun W'' => ?_, naturality := ?_ }
311	  · -- app component at `W''` (over `fV`).  `W' := (unop W'').left ≤ fV`; `P := f⁻¹ᵁ W'`.
312	    -- The down-set facts are established (axiom-clean); the morphism itself is the documented

347	    -- **Cross-fiber transport — CLOSED (iter-303).**  The goal lives in `ModuleCat 𝒪_X(W')` but
348	    -- `core` lives in `ModuleCat 𝒪_X(fP)` (`fP = f''ᵁf⁻¹ᵁW'`, propositionally `= W'` via `he`, but
349	    -- the section RINGS `𝒪_X(W')` / `𝒪_X(fP)` are only propositionally equal).  The source relabel

391	/-- **Leg (A)∘(B): the sectionwise slice transport of the dual along an open immersion.**
392	
393	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X`, `M : X.Modules`, and an open `V` of `Y` (as `(Opens Y)ᵒᵖ`),
394	this is the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear isomorphism between the two sectionwise values of the Step-4 residual
395	of `dual_restrict_iso`:
396	```
397	  ((pushforward β).obj (dual M.val)).obj V  ≅  (dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V
398	```
399	where `β` is the open-immersion structure ring morphism `Y.ringCatSheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙
400	X.ringCatSheaf` (`β.app U = (forget₂ _ _).map (f.appIso U).inv`).
401	
402	The construction mirrors `homLocalSection` (the thin-poset `eqToHom`-conjugation slice transport)
403	composed with `restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv` (the `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear codomain-unit ring swap of leg
404	(B)): a dual section `φ : restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X` over `Over (fV)` is reindexed across
405	`f.opensFunctor` to a dual section over `Over V`, conjugating each component by the structure ring
406	iso `f.appIso`; naturality on the thin poset `Opens Y` is `Subsingleton.elim`. -/
407	noncomputable def sliceDualTransport {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f]
408	    (M : X.Modules) (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥Y)ᵒᵖ) :
409	    letI α : Y.presheaf ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
410	      { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
411	    letI β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ (Hom.opensFunctor f).op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
412	      Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
413	    (((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj (PresheafOfModules.dual M.val)).obj V) ≅
414	      ((PresheafOfModules.dual ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward β).obj M.val)).obj V) := by
415	  -- CONSTRUCTION PLAN (homLocalSection-style leg (A) ∘ restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv leg (B)):
416	  --
417	  -- Write `fV := f.opensFunctor.obj V.unop`.  By `PresheafOfModules.pushforward_obj_obj`,
418	  --   LHS carrier `L = (dual M.val).obj (op fV) = (restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X)`,
419	  --     a `𝒪_X(fV)`-module restricted along `β.app V : 𝒪_Y(V) ⟶ 𝒪_X(fV)` to a `𝒪_Y(V)`-module;
420	  --   RHS carrier `Rr = (restr V.unop ((pushforward β).obj M.val) ⟶ restr V.unop 𝟙_Y)`,
421	  --     a `𝒪_Y(V)`-module via `internalHomObjModule`.
422	  --
423	  -- Build a `𝒪_Y(V)`-linear equivalence `e : L ≃ₗ[𝒪_Y(V)] Rr` and return `e.toModuleIso`.
424	  --
425	  -- `e.toFun φ` (for `φ : restr fV M.val ⟶ restr fV 𝟙_X`) is the dual section over `Over V`
426	  -- whose component at `W : (Over V.unop)ᵒᵖ` (so `W' := W.unop.left ≤ V.unop`, with image
427	  -- `fW' := f.opensFunctor.obj W'`) is

728	noncomputable def dual_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
729	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M : X.Modules) :
730	    (dual M).restrict f ≅ dual (M.restrict f) := by
731	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`.
732	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (dual M) ≪≫ ?_
733	  -- Step 2. Sheafification commutes with pullback.
734	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
735	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) ≪≫ ?_
736	  -- Step 3. Strip the outer sheafification, descending to the presheaf residual.
737	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf)
738	      (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
739	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL): the presheaf goal
740	  --   `(pullback φ).obj (dual M.val) ≅ dual ((M.restrict f).val)`.
741	  -- H1: replace `pullback φ` with `pushforward β` (β the open-immersion structure ring iso).
742	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
743	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
744	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
745	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
746	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
747	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
748	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
749	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
750	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
751	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
752	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_
753	  -- Residual: `(pushforward β).obj (dual M.val) ≅ dual ((pushforward β).obj M.val)`.

12	# The `Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` substrate (A.1.c.SubT)
13	
14	This file is the **A.1.c.SubT** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
15	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It records the dedicated
16	substrate on which the abelian-group instance of the relative Picard quotient
17	`Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) := Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` rests (the residual `sorry`
18	of `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`).
19	
20	The mathematics is straightforward; the obstacle to formalisation is purely
21	infrastructural. The Lean construction of the abelian-group law
22	`[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` on isomorphism classes of line bundles requires three
23	ingredients on the Lean carrier:
24	
25	1. a binary tensor-product operation
26	   `⊗ : Scheme.Modules X × Scheme.Modules X → Scheme.Modules X`;
27	2. the structure sheaf `O_X` as a designated unit object for `⊗`;
28	3. an inverse operation on the full subcategory of invertible objects, i.e.
29	   the dual `L⁻¹ = Hom(L, O_X)` of an invertible sheaf.
30	
31	At Mathlib's pinned commit (`b80f227`), only a presheaf-level version of (1)
32	is available (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`); (2) and (3) are present
33	as scheme-level objects, but the binary operation in (1) that ties them
34	together at the `Scheme.Modules` level is missing, and there is no
35	`MonoidalCategory` instance on `Scheme.Modules X`. This file records the
36	project-side substrate that supplies (1) and consequently lifts (2) + (3)
37	into a monoidal-category structure on `Scheme.Modules X`.
38

154	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
155	
156	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
157	
158	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
159	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
160	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
161	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
162	
163	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
164	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
165	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
166	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
167	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
168	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
169	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
170	
171	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
172	
173	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
174	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
175	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
176	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
177	
178	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
179	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
180	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
181	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
182	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
183	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
184	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
185	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
186	
187	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
188	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
189	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
190	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
191	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
192	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
193	law is definitional. -/
194	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
195	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
196	
197	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
198	
199	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
200	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the
201	underlying presheaf of `M` (the internal hom into the structure presheaf,
202	`M^∨(U) = ℋom_{𝒪_X|_U}(M|_U, 𝒪_X|_U)`).
203	
204	Construction = the **exact dual analogue of `tensorObj`** (this file, `tensorObj`):
205	apply the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 …)` on the
206	small Zariski site of `X` to the (axiom-clean, sub-step-3) presheaf dual
207	`PresheafOfModules.dual M.val`. The scheme's structure presheaf `X.presheaf` is
208	`CommRingCat`-valued over the single-universe topological site `Opens X`, hence is
209	exactly the base `R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}` that `PresheafOfModules.dual`
210	requires (the value `M^∨(U) = M|_U ⟶ R|_U` is an `R(U)`-module, needing
211	commutativity) — no CommRingCat/RingCat re-bridging is needed, since
212	`tensorObj` already takes `(R := X.presheaf)` over the same CommRingCat presheaf
213	and `X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` definitionally.
214	
215	The sheafification functor already lands in `SheafOfModules`, so no manual
216	`Presheaf.IsSheaf` / sheaf-condition descent is needed (sheafifying an already-sheaf
217	gives an iso object; this is the file's convention, matching `tensorObj`).
218	
219	Per blueprint `lem:internal_hom_isSheaf` (§`sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`); Stacks
220	tags 01CM (internal hom into a sheaf is a sheaf) / 01CR item 2. This is the
221	`⊗`-inverse candidate of an invertible sheaf, feeding `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
222	noncomputable def dual {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
223	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
224	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) :
225	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
226	
227	/-- **The sheaf-level dual is contravariantly functorial in isomorphisms.** An isomorphism
228	`e : M ≅ M'` in `X.Modules` induces `dual M' ≅ dual M`, obtained by sheafifying the presheaf-level
229	dual iso `PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso` of the underlying presheaf isomorphism. This is the
230	reusable "dual respects isos" ingredient (the dual analogue of `tensorObjIsoOfIso`) feeding the
231	assembly of `dual_isLocallyTrivial`: a trivialisation `L.restrict f ≅ 𝒪` yields, contravariantly,
232	`dual 𝒪 ≅ dual (L.restrict f)`. -/
233	noncomputable def dualIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules} (e : M ≅ M') :
234	    dual M' ≅ dual M :=
235	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
236	    (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso (R₀ := X.presheaf)
237	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e))
238	
239	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
240	
241	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
242	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
243	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
244	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
245	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
246	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
247	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
248	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
249	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
250	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
251	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
252	
253	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
254	
255	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
256	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
257	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
258	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
259	
260	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
261	
262	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
263	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
264	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
265	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
266	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
267	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
268	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
269	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
270	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
271	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
272	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
273	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
274	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
275	
276	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
277	sheaf.**
278	
279	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
280	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
281	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
282	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
283	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
284	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
285	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
286	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
287	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
288	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
289	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
290	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
291	
292	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
293	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
294	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
295	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
296	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
297	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
298	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
299	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
300	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
301	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
302	
303	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
304	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
305	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the
306	sheafification counit. -/
307	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
308	    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅ M :=
309	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
310	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
311	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
312	
313	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
314	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
315	The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `β_ M.val
316	N.val` sheafifies to the asserted isomorphism by the cheap `mapIso` pattern. -/
317	noncomputable def tensorObj_braiding {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) :
318	    tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj N M :=
319	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
320	    (BraidedCategory.braiding
321	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) M.val N.val)
322	
323	/-- **Associator for `⊗_X`.** (Blueprint
324	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso`.)
325	For arbitrary `M, N, P : X.Modules` there is an isomorphism
326	`(M ⊗_X N) ⊗_X P ≅ M ⊗_X (N ⊗_X P)`. This is the objectwise existence-of-iso datum the
327	group law consumes (associativity as a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)`).
328	
329	**UNCONDITIONAL and axiom-clean (iter-238 ROUTE (d)).** No flatness or local-triviality
330	hypothesis is used: the earlier flatness route (`W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_flat`, needing
331	sectionwise flatness — false for a general line bundle on a non-affine open) is RETIRED.
332	Writing `a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)` and `η` the
333	sheafification-adjunction unit, the three-step composite is:
334	  1. `a(η_{M.val ⊗ᵖ N.val} ▷ P.val)` is iso, giving
335	     `(M ⊗ N) ⊗ P ≅ a((M.val⊗N.val) ⊗ P.val)`;
336	  2. `a.mapIso α : a((M.val⊗N.val)⊗P.val) ≅ a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val))`, `α` the
337	     presheaf-of-modules associator;
338	  3. `a(M.val ◁ η_{N.val ⊗ᵖ P.val})` is iso, giving
339	     `a(M.val⊗(N.val⊗P.val)) ≅ M ⊗ (N ⊗ P)`.
340	Steps 1/3 invert the whiskered sheafification unit via the flatness-free
341	`PresheafOfModules.W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W` (η = `toSheafify ∈ J.W`, and `J.W` is
342	stable under whiskering) together with `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W` (the sheafification
343	functor IS the localization at `J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf _)`). The defeq carrier bridge
344	`X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` is handled by the leading
345	`letI instMS` below. -/
346	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules} :
347	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) := by
348	  -- UNCONDITIONAL (iter-238, step 0): the locally-trivial hypotheses are dropped —
349	  -- the body never consumed them (the whiskered-unit localizer fact holds for
350	  -- arbitrary modules under ROUTE (d)). Matches the blueprint `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso`
351	  -- framed unconditional and enables `tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible`.
352	  -- Bridge the monoidal structure across the `rfl`-defeq carrier
353	  -- `Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`.
354	  letI instMS : MonoidalCategoryStruct (_root_.PresheafOfModules (Sheaf.val X.ringCatSheaf)) :=
355	    inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategoryStruct
356	      (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))
357	  set a := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) with ha
358	  -- Underlying presheaf tensors and the sheafification unit `η = toSheafify`.
359	  set MN := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val with hMN
360	  set NP := PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) N.val P.val with hNP
361	  set η := (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction
362	    (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).unit with hη
363	  -- The two whiskered-unit localizer facts (ROUTE (d), via `W_whisker{Right,Left}_of_W`).
364	  -- `η_A = toSheafify` lies in `J.W` (`W_toSheafify`).
365	  have hηMN : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
366	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN)) := by
367	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
368	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
369	  have hηNP : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
370	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app NP)) := by
371	    rw [hη, PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app]
372	    exact CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology.W_toSheafify _ _
373	  have hW1 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
374	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (η.app MN ▷ P.val)) :=
375	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_W (R := X.presheaf) P.val (η.app MN) hηMN
376	  have hW3 : (Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
377	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _).map (M.val ◁ η.app NP)) :=
378	    PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W (R := X.presheaf) M.val (η.app NP) hηNP
379	  -- Steps 1 and 3: the sheafification functor inverts the whiskered units.
380	  have hi1 : IsIso (a.map (η.app MN ▷ P.val)) :=
381	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW1
382	  have hi3 : IsIso (a.map (M.val ◁ η.app NP)) :=
383	    PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val) _ hW3
384	  -- Step 2: the presheaf-of-modules associator, transported under `a`.
385	  have e2 := a.mapIso
386	    ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).associator M.val N.val P.val)
387	  exact (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi1).symm ≪≫ e2 ≪≫ (@asIso _ _ _ _ _ hi3)
388	
389	/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
390	open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.
391	
392	The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
393	factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
394	and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
395	`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
396	`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
397	via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
398	because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
399	noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
400	    (hWU : W ≤ U)
401	    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
402	    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
403	  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
404	  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
405	  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
406	  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
407	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
408	  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
409	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
410	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
411	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
412	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
413	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
414	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
415	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
416	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
417	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
418	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
419	  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
420	  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
421	  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI
422	
423	/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**
424	
425	For an open immersion `f : Y ⟶ X` and `M N : X.Modules`,
426	`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.
427	
428	This is the single substrate linchpin of `A.1.c.SubT` — **CLOSED, axiom-clean**
429	(iter-217). It says the substrate `⊗` (sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules
430	tensor) commutes with the restriction functor along an open immersion. The proof
431	is the blueprint's four-step composite:
432	  Step 1 (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`): reduce `restrict` to the abstract pullback.
433	  Step 2 (`SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`): move the pullback inside the
434	    sheafification (sheafification commutes with pullback).
435	  Step 3: strip the outer sheafification (`.mapIso`), descending to the presheaf goal
436	    `(pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val) ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`.
437	  Step 4: close that presheaf goal by **H1 ∘ H2**:
438	    • H1 (the sole Mathlib-ABSENT ingredient, BUILT this iter): the presheaf-level iso
439	      `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, obtained from the de-sheafified presheaf
440	      `PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (adjunction along the open-immersion
441	      pair `f.opensFunctor ⊣ Opens.map f.base`) against the existing
442	      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
443	      `restrictFunctor` structure map, so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`
444	      definitionally.
445	    • H2 (strong-monoidal tensorator): `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`
446	      with `β` sectionwise the open-immersion ring ISO `f.appIso`, so `restrictScalars β`
447	      is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`, resting on the closed
448	      `restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv` / `restrictScalars_isIso_{μ,ε}`); the composite
449	      `μIso` is the tensorator.
450	The superseded `Localization.Monoidal` / `J.W.IsMonoidal` route is NOT used. -/
451	noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
452	    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
453	    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f) := by
454	  -- Step 1. Reduce `restrict` to `pullback` along the open immersion `f`
455	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
456	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
457	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
458	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj
459	  -- M.val N.val)`, so the genuine Mathlib lemma
460	  -- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`
461	  -- (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`,
462	  -- `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅ PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙
463	  -- sheafification`) moves the pullback *inside* the sheafification. This
464	  -- discharges half (ii) of the original obstruction (sheafification commuting
465	  -- with pullback). After it the goal is the purely **presheaf-level** residual
466	  -- `sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
467	  --    ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)`.
468	  refine (SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom).app
469	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val) ≪≫ ?_
470	  -- Step 3. **Strip the outer sheafification.** Both sides are
471	  -- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)` applied to a
472	  -- presheaf-of-modules: the LHS to `(pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)`, and
473	  -- the RHS `(M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)` *by definition* to
474	  -- `(M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`. So it suffices to give the
475	  -- comparison at the PRESHEAF level and sheafify it. This is a genuine reduction
476	  -- step (verified: the goal below has no sheafification).
477	  refine (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.obj)).mapIso ?_
478	  -- Step 4 (RESIDUAL CLOSURE — iter-217 H1 build). The remaining presheaf goal is
479	  --   `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj (M.val ⊗ₚ N.val)
480	  --      ≅ (M.restrict f).val ⊗ₚ (N.restrict f).val`
481	  -- where `φ = (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` and `⊗ₚ =
482	  -- PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`. We close it via:
483	  --  (H1, the linchpin) the presheaf-level iso `pushforward β ≅ pullback φ`, built from
484	  --      the presheaf `pushforwardPushforwardAdj` (above) against the existing
485	  --      `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` via `leftAdjointUniq`. Here `β` is the
486	  --      open-immersion structure map of `restrictFunctor f`, so
487	  --      `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val` definitionally.
488	  --  (H2) the strong-monoidal comparison `(pushforward β).obj (A ⊗ₚ B) ≅
489	  --      (pushforward β).obj A ⊗ₚ (pushforward β).obj B`.
490	  -- `φR` (the scheme structure map) and `β` (the restrictFunctor structure map) are kept as
491	  -- `let`-bindings (zeta-transparent) so the unit/counit triangle goals below reduce; the
492	  -- open-immersion adjunction is INLINED for the same reason (a `have` would make `adj.unit`
493	  -- opaque and block the `congr` defeq, exactly as in Mathlib's sheaf-level `restrictAdjunction`).
494	  let φR := (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom
495	  -- The restrictFunctor structure map `β` (so `(M.restrict f).val = (pushforward β).obj M.val`).
496	  let α : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf :=
497	    { app := fun U => (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
498	  let β : Y.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
499	    Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
500	  -- H1 via the presheaf pushforward-pushforward adjunction + `leftAdjointUniq`.
501	  have hadj : PresheafOfModules.pushforward β ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward φR :=
502	    PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction β φR
503	      (by ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x))
504	      (by ext U x; exact congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x))
505	  let H1 := hadj.leftAdjointUniq (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φR)
506	  refine (H1.app (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)).symm ≪≫ ?_
507	  -- H2: the strong-monoidal tensorator of `pushforward β = pushforward₀ ⋙ restrictScalars β`.
508	  -- `β` is sectionwise bijective (it is the `forget₂`-image of the open-immersion structure ring
509	  -- ISO `f.appIso`), so `restrictScalars β` is STRONG monoidal (`restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective`),
510	  -- and `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` is `Monoidal` (Mathlib); the composite's `μIso` is the tensorator.
511	  -- It is built over the SYNTACTIC `_ ⋙ forget₂` base form (where the `MonoidalCategory` instance is
512	  -- found canonically); the result is DEFEQ to the goal — whose base `X.ringCatSheaf.obj` is only
513	  -- defeq, not syntactically, `X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂` — and `(pushforward β).obj M.val =
514	  -- (M.restrict f).val` definitionally, so `exact` closes it without any instance diamond.
515	  have hβ : ∀ U, Function.Bijective (β.app U).hom := by
516	    intro U
517	    haveI : IsIso (β.app U) :=
518	      inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat).map (f.appIso U.unop).inv))
519	    exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso (β.app U)
520	  let β' : (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
521	      (f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ X.presheaf) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := β
522	  haveI : (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β').Monoidal :=
523	    PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective β' hβ
524	  exact (Functor.Monoidal.μIso
525	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat f.opensFunctor X.presheaf
526	      ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars β')
527	    (M.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
528	    (N.val : _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))).symm
529	
530	/-- **Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial.**
531	
532	If `M, N : X.Modules` are locally trivial of rank one (line bundles), so is
533	their tensor product `tensorObj M N`. Per blueprint
534	`lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial`. The proof picks, for each point `x`,
535	a common affine open `W ∋ x` contained in trivialising opens `U` (for `M`) and
536	`U'` (for `N`), refines both trivialisations to `W` via `restrictIsoUnitOfLE`,
537	then transports through `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, the bifunctoriality
538	`tensorObjIsoOfIso`, and the unit isomorphism `tensorObj_unit_iso`:
539	`(M ⊗ N)|_W ≅ M|_W ⊗ N|_W ≅ 𝒪_W ⊗ 𝒪_W ≅ 𝒪_W`. The only residual gap is the
540	substrate-restriction compatibility `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. -/
541	lemma tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
542	    (hM : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial M) (hN : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial N) :
543	    LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial (tensorObj M N) := by
544	  intro x
545	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU_aff, ⟨eM⟩⟩ := hM x
546	  obtain ⟨U', hxU', hU'_aff, ⟨eN⟩⟩ := hN x
547	  obtain ⟨W, hW_aff, hxW, hWsub⟩ :=
548	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := x) (U := U ⊓ U') ⟨hxU, hxU'⟩
549	  have hWU : W ≤ U := le_trans hWsub inf_le_left
550	  have hWU' : W ≤ U' := le_trans hWsub inf_le_right
551	  refine ⟨W, hxW, hW_aff, ⟨?_⟩⟩
552	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
553	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫
554	    tensorObj_unit_iso

556	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the d.2-free descent re-route (B-connector)
557	
558	The "locally-iso ⇒ iso" half of the descent assembly of `exists_tensorObj_inverse`:
559	a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on an open
560	neighbourhood of every point is a global isomorphism. The route is the stalkwise
561	iso criterion `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (for sheaves valued
562	in `Ab`, whose forgetful functor reflects isos and preserves limits / filtered
563	colimits) together with `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso` (restriction along an
564	open immersion commutes with stalks). **No stalk-⊗ ("d.2") is invoked**: this is a
565	statement about a single module morphism, never about the tensor stalk. -/
566	
567	/-- **B-connector: a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on
568	an open cover is an isomorphism.** For `φ : M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`, if every point
569	`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ`
570	is an isomorphism, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This is the B-bridge of the d.2-free
571	descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
572	lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
573	    (U : X → X.Opens) (hxU : ∀ x, x ∈ U x)

606	/-- **A-bridge step (ii): promote an `𝒪_X`-linear `Ab`-presheaf morphism to a module
607	morphism.** Given a morphism `g : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf` of the underlying
608	`Ab`-presheaves that is sectionwise `𝒪_X`-linear, package it as a morphism `M ⟶ N`
609	of `𝒪_X`-modules. This wraps `PresheafOfModules.homMk` at the `Scheme.Modules` level;
610	it is the "promote to `𝒪_X`-linear" half of the descent A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat`
611	(the ab-sheaf gluing produces the linear `g`; this lemma turns it into a module map).
612	Sectionwise linearity is a property the consumer checks on a separated presheaf. -/
613	noncomputable def homMk {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
614	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
615	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
616	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
617	    M ⟶ N :=
618	  ⟨PresheafOfModules.homMk (M₁ := M.val) (M₂ := N.val) g hg⟩

622	    (g : M.val.presheaf ⟶ N.val.presheaf)
623	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),
624	      (g.app V).hom (r • m) = r • (g.app V).hom m) :
625	    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map (homMk g hg) = g :=
626	  rfl

753	/-- **Step 3 — `⊗`-invertibility is closed under tensor product** (blueprint
754	`lem:isinvertible_tensor`). If `M, M'` are `⊗`-invertible with inverses `N, N'`,
755	then `N ⊗ N'` is a tensor inverse of `M ⊗ M'`. -/
756	theorem IsInvertible.tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules}
757	    (hM : IsInvertible M) (hM' : IsInvertible M') :
758	    IsInvertible (Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M M') := by
759	  obtain ⟨N, ⟨e⟩⟩ := hM
760	  obtain ⟨N', ⟨e'⟩⟩ := hM'
761	  exact ⟨Scheme.Modules.tensorObj N N',
762	    ⟨tensorObj_middleFour M M' N N' ≪≫ tensorObjIsoOfIso e e' ≪≫ tensorObj_unit_iso⟩⟩
763	
764	/-- **Step 4 — the structure sheaf is `⊗`-invertible** (blueprint
765	`lem:isinvertible_unit`). Witness `𝒪_X`, iso `tensorObj_unit_iso`. -/
766	theorem isInvertible_unit {X : Scheme.{u}} :
767	    IsInvertible (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :=
768	  ⟨SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf, ⟨tensorObj_unit_iso⟩⟩
769	
770	/-- **Step 5 — the tensor inverse is determined up to isomorphism** (blueprint
771	`lem:isinvertible_inverse_welldef`). If `M ⊗ N ≅ 𝒪_X` and `M ⊗ N' ≅ 𝒪_X` then
772	`N ≅ N'`, via the inverse-of-inverse chain. -/
773	theorem IsInvertible.inverse_unique {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N N' : X.Modules}
774	    (e : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
775	    (e' : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N' ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :
776	    Nonempty (N ≅ N') :=
777	  ⟨(tensorObj_right_unitor N).symm ≪≫
778	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl N) e'.symm ≪≫
779	    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm ≪≫
780	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding N M ≪≫ e) (Iso.refl N') ≪≫
781	    tensorObj_left_unitor N'⟩

783	/-- The setoid of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules: `M ∼ M'` iff there exists an
784	isomorphism `M ≅ M'` (blueprint `def:pic_carrier`). -/
785	instance picSetoid (X : Scheme.{u}) : Setoid {M : X.Modules // IsInvertible M} where
786	  r M M' := Nonempty ((M : X.Modules) ≅ (M' : X.Modules))
787	  iseqv :=
788	    ⟨fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ ⟨f⟩ => ⟨e ≪≫ f⟩⟩
789	
790	/-- **Step 2 — the invertibility-carrier Picard group** (blueprint
791	`def:pic_carrier`): the quotient of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules by isomorphism. -/
792	def PicGroup (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ := Quotient (picSetoid X)
793	
794	/-- Multiplication on `PicGroup X`: `[M] · [M'] := [M ⊗_X M']`, well-defined on
795	iso-classes by bifunctoriality (`tensorObjIsoOfIso`), landing in `PicGroup` by
796	`IsInvertible.tensorObj`. -/
797	noncomputable def picMul {X : Scheme.{u}} : PicGroup X → PicGroup X → PicGroup X :=
798	  Quotient.lift₂
799	    (fun a b => Quotient.mk _ ⟨tensorObj a.1 b.1, a.2.tensorObj b.2⟩)
800	    (by
801	      rintro ⟨a, ha⟩ ⟨b, hb⟩ ⟨a', ha'⟩ ⟨b', hb'⟩ ⟨ea⟩ ⟨eb⟩
802	      exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObjIsoOfIso ea eb⟩)
803	
804	/-- The inverse class on `PicGroup X`: `[M] ↦ [N]` for the membership witness `N`
805	of `IsInvertible M`, well-defined by `IsInvertible.inverse_unique`. -/
806	noncomputable def picInv {X : Scheme.{u}} : PicGroup X → PicGroup X :=
807	  Quotient.lift
808	    (fun a => Quotient.mk _
809	      ⟨Classical.choose a.2,
810	        a.1, ⟨tensorObj_braiding _ a.1 ≪≫ (Classical.choose_spec a.2).some⟩⟩)
811	    (by
812	      rintro ⟨a, ha⟩ ⟨a', ha'⟩ ⟨ea⟩
813	      refine Quotient.sound ?_
814	      -- both `Classical.choose ha` and `Classical.choose ha'` are tensor inverses of `a`
815	      have h1 : tensorObj a (Classical.choose ha) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
816	        (Classical.choose_spec ha).some
817	      have h2 : tensorObj a (Classical.choose ha') ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
818	        tensorObjIsoOfIso ea (Iso.refl _) ≪≫ (Classical.choose_spec ha').some
819	      exact IsInvertible.inverse_unique h1 h2)

821	/-- **Step 6 — the invertibility-carrier Picard group is abelian** (blueprint
822	`thm:pic_commgroup`). `[M] · [M'] := [M ⊗_X M']`, `1 := [𝒪_X]`, and `[M]⁻¹` the
823	class of any membership witness of `IsInvertible M`. Each group axiom is a single
824	existence-of-isomorphism: unit laws ← unitors, associativity ← associator,
825	commutativity ← braiding, left inverse ← the witness iso. No monoidal coherence. -/
826	noncomputable instance picCommGroup (X : Scheme.{u}) : CommGroup (PicGroup X) where
827	  mul := picMul
828	  one := Quotient.mk _ ⟨SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf, isInvertible_unit⟩
829	  inv := picInv
830	  mul_assoc := by
831	    rintro a b c
832	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
833	    induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ b => ?_
834	    induction c using Quotient.ind with | _ c => ?_
835	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObj_assoc_iso⟩
836	  one_mul := by
837	    rintro a
838	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
839	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObj_left_unitor a.1⟩
840	  mul_one := by
841	    rintro a
842	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
843	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObj_right_unitor a.1⟩
844	  inv_mul_cancel := by
845	    rintro a
846	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
847	    exact Quotient.sound
848	      ⟨tensorObj_braiding (Classical.choose a.2) a.1 ≪≫ (Classical.choose_spec a.2).some⟩
849	  mul_comm := by
850	    rintro a b
851	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
852	    induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ b => ?_
853	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObj_braiding a.1 b.1⟩

891	For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X`, the canonical comparison

1003	/-! ### Phase 1 — `pullbackUnitIso` (`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`), blueprint `lem:pullback_unit_iso`
1004	
1005	**iter-241 RESOLUTION (the chart-chase is NOT needed).** The unit comparison
1006	`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an isomorphism for *every* morphism of
1007	schemes `f`, not just for `Final`-chart morphisms. The Mathlib instance
1008	`SheafOfModules.instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal` fires whenever the comparison
1009	functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`, and that functor is **always** `Final`: the
1010	preimage functor on opens preserves finite limits (it is a frame homomorphism), so it is
1011	representably flat, whence `final_of_representablyFlat` supplies `(Opens.map f.base).Final`
1012	unconditionally. (Verified axiom-clean for a general `f`.) The elaborate affine
1013	chart-chase contemplated by the blueprint proof — and the iter-240 coherence linchpin
1014	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` — are therefore unnecessary for this lemma (the linchpin is
1015	retained above as it is the genuine Mathlib-absent pseudofunctor coherence, of independent
1016	use for the harder Phase-2 tensor comparison).
1017	
1018	The remaining friction was purely a Lean typeclass-resolution accident: in a context with
1019	several `(Opens.map _).Final` hypotheses (or after a `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` rewrite)
1020	the buried implicit instance args of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (`[F.IsContinuous]`,
1021	`[(pushforward φ).IsRightAdjoint]`) are defeq-but-not-syntactic, so `asIso`/`infer_instance`
1022	fails to synthesise `IsIso (pbu f)`. The fix (mathlib-analogist `pbu-canon`, the
1023	`Functor.Monoidal.μIso` idiom): isolate the single `Final` hypothesis in the helper
1024	`isIso_pbu_of_final` whose body `inferInstance` runs at a clean site, then transport the
1025	resulting witness through `asIso` by passing it *explicitly* (`@asIso … (isIso_pbu_of_final g)`)
1026	— the application's defeq check runs at default transparency and succeeds, whereas instance
1027	synthesis (reducible transparency) does not. -/
1028	
1029	/-- **`IsIso (pullbackObjUnitToUnit g)` from a single `Final` hypothesis, at a clean site.**
1030	Project-local: isolates the lone `(Opens.map g.base).Final` instance so that the Mathlib
1031	`OfFinal` instance synthesises without colliding with other in-scope `Final`/`IsIso`
1032	hypotheses (see the section note). -/
1033	private lemma isIso_pbu_of_final {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
1034	    [(TopologicalSpace.Opens.map g.base).Final] :
1035	    IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit g.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
1036	
1037	/-- **Bundled `Iso` form of the unit comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit g`** for a `Final`
1038	comparison functor — the analogue of `CategoryTheory.Functor.Monoidal.μIso`. Project-local:
1039	hands out the isomorphism (rather than a bare `IsIso` instance) so downstream coherence
1040	reasoning stays at the `Iso` level and never re-triggers the `pbu` instance synthesis. -/

1264	topological inverse image `pullback₀`. -/
1265	private lemma pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1266	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀.{u} F R).IsRightAdjoint :=
1267	  inferInstanceAs (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))).IsRightAdjoint
1268	
1269	/-- `restrictScalars φ` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`.
1270	Project-local; carries the existence of the extension of scalars `extendScalars`. -/
1271	private lemma restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1272	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
1273	  inferInstanceAs
1274	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (F := 𝟭 C) (R := F.op ⋙ R) φ).IsRightAdjoint
1275	
1276	/-- **The topological inverse image `pullback₀ := (pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint`**, the
1277	left adjoint of the fixed-ring pushforward. On underlying presheaves it is the left Kan
1278	extension along `F.op`. Project-local (no `PresheafOfModules` inverse-image functor at the
1279	pin); the carrier of D3. -/
1280	noncomputable def pullback0 (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1281	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} R :=
1282	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1283	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint
1284	
1285	/-- **Extension of scalars `extendScalars φ := (restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint`**, the left
1286	adjoint of restriction of scalars along a morphism of ring presheaves. Project-local; the
1287	carrier of D2 (strong monoidal via `distribBaseChange`). -/
1288	noncomputable def extendScalars (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1289	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} S ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) :=
1290	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1291	  (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint
1292	
1293	/-- The adjunction `pullback₀ ⊣ pushforward₀`. -/
1294	noncomputable def pullback0Adjunction (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1295	    pullback0 F R ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R :=
1296	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1297	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R)
1298	
1299	/-- The adjunction `extendScalars φ ⊣ restrictScalars φ`. -/
1300	noncomputable def extendScalarsAdjunction (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1301	    extendScalars φ ⊣ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ :=
1302	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1303	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ)
1304	
1305	/-- **D1 — the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition** (blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition`).
1306	For `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` presenting a pushforward of presheaves of modules, the presheaf
1307	pullback factors as extension of scalars followed by the topological inverse image,
1308	`pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀`. This is the left-adjoint reversal of the
1309	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ`, obtained
1310	from `Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso` (uniqueness of left adjoints). Project-local. -/
1311	noncomputable def pullbackLanDecomposition (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1312	    PresheafOfModules.pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback0 F R :=
1313	  (Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso
1314	    (extendScalarsAdjunction φ) (pullback0Adjunction F R)
1315	    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ)
1316	    (Iso.refl (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ))).symm
1317	
1318	end PullbackLanDecomposition

1320	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — D1'–D4' loc-triv pullback–tensor comparison
1321	
1322	The locally-trivial-restricted upgrade of the oplax comparison map
1323	`pullbackTensorMap` (`f^*(M ⊗ N) ⟶ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`) to an isomorphism, blueprint
1324	§`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`, sub-lemmas D1'–D4'. -/
1325	
1326	section LocTrivPullbackTensor
1327	
1328	/-- **The sheafified `⊗ₘ` of the two `pullbackValIso`s (piece 4 of `pullbackTensorMap`) is an
1329	iso.** Factored out as a top-level lemma so the `tensorIso (C := _ ⋙ forget₂)` elaboration mirrors
1330	the proven `tensorObjIsoOfIso` (it does not elaborate cleanly inside a tactic block carrying
1331	`extract_lets` locals). Project-local helper for `isIso_pullbackTensorMap_of_isIso_sheafifyDelta`. -/
1332	private lemma isIso_sheafify_tensorHom_pullbackValIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}}
1333	    (f : Y ⟶ X) (M N : X.Modules) :
1334	    IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1335	      (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1336	        (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1337	        ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f M).hom)
1338	        ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).map (pullbackValIso f N).hom))) :=
1339	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := Y.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
1340	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
1341	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (Y.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
1342	      ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso (pullbackValIso f M))
1343	      ((SheafOfModules.forget Y.ringCatSheaf).mapIso (pullbackValIso f N)))).isIso_hom
